top_signals.title: FTSE 100 Live: Stocks slump as Middle East strikes dim peace hopes; Tech sell-off rocks investors
top_signals.summary: Good morning and welcome back to the City AM liveblog. An exchange of fire from Iran and Israel has left markets returning from the weekend with little hope a fully-fledged peace deal with the US could be on the horizon. The Israel Defence Forces (IDF) said it had fired strikes at military targets in western and [...]

top_signals.title: What Beirut’s Port Scanners Miss About Militant Supply Chains
top_signals.summary:
At the Port of Beirut, the new scanners did exactly what they were built to do. They saw the lithium batteries. They saw the drone propellers. They saw the fiber optic cable. They matched the scans against the paperwork, found no obvious deception, and cleared the cargo.That was the problem.The threat was not hidden in any single container. It was spread across many of them, arriving over weeks, through different vessels, different companies, and different bills of lading. The AI could identify what each shipment contained, but couldn’t figure out what those shipments, taken together, might be building toward.As a

The post What Beirut’s Port Scanners Miss About Militant Supply Chains appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: ‘Why single out banks?’: Santander chief hits out at UK tax regime
top_signals.summary: The boss of Santander has branded the UK’s tax regime on banks as making “no economic sense” as speculation mounts that the industry could be turned to for a quick cash grab if the Labour government lurches to the left. Ana Botín, the chief executive of the Spanish banking giant, has said the UK tax [...]

top_signals.title: Okinawa schools adopting ‘learncation’ to give students more family time
top_signals.summary: The program allows children to take authorized leave from school on weekdays to spend time with family for educational activities.

top_signals.title: Victims mourned 18 years after Akihabara stabbing incident
top_signals.summary: The attacker, then 25, rammed a truck into a pedestrian area near Akihabara Station and hit passers-by before attacking them with a dagger.
